rulings.Case Title: MARTINA MUELLER VS. KLAUS KUBLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Of Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 10, 2010, line 1. PLAINTIFF MARTINA MUELLER'S Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Of Settlement is granted in part. The settlement amount and the payment plan is found to be in good faith under C.C.P. section 877.6(a), notwithstanding the provision regarding allocation of damages. The court further finds that the allocation of damages in a settlement agreement or determined by a court at a C.C.P. section 877.6 hearing are not binding at the time of trial. Greathouse v. Amcord (1995) 35 Cal. App. 4th 831, 841; Gouvis Engineering v. Superior Court (1995) 37 Cal. App. 4th 642, 650-651. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARK HAMMOND VS. CHRISTOPHER SOAREZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 10, 2010, line 2. DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER SUAREZ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof and plaintiff raises no triable issue of material fact. The litigation privilege is not applicable, however the release bars this action. Paragraph 8 of the release states that plaintiff releases all claims except those stated in paragraph 10. Paragraph 10 only applies where defendant's family moves from the premises within 36 months of April 3, 2007. Plaintiff and his counsel agreed to that narrow exception and it does not apply to the instant case. The claims of duress, undue influence, and unconscionability fail. Plaintiff had counsel and received $20,000 in exchange for the release. The exception at paragraph 10 is not ambiguous and parole evidence is disallowed. The exception is not reasonably susceptible to plaintiff's proffered meaning that he retained all rights under the S F A C and was allowed to sue if defendant's family did not move into the premises within a certain time. Plaintiff's claim of fraud fails because a party cannot rely on the fraud exception to the parole evidence rule where the alleged misrepresentation contradicts an express term. See Banco Do Brazil, S.A. v. Lation, Inc. (1991) 234 Cal. App. 3d 973, 1009. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: TERROIR HOTEL AND RESORT FUND, L.P.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of: 1) Petitioner'S Intention To Move For New Trial And 2) Hearing On Petitioner'S Mtn For A New Trial And Mtn To Amend Order Confirming Arbitration Award Or, Alt., To Vacate Order And Enter Different Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 10, 2010, line 12. PETITIONER TERROIR HOTEL AND RESORT FUND, L.P.'S Notice Of: 1) Petitioner's Intention To Move For New Trial And 2) Hearing On Petitioner's Motion For A New Trial And Motion To Amend Order Confirming Arbitration Award Or, Alt., To Vacate Order And Enter Different Order. Motion denied. Reply is stricken as violative of motion for new trial procedures. (C.R.C. 3.1600, C.C.P. section 661). The arbitrator's award does not constitute damages or a money judgment, but rather the creation of an executory contract. Furthermore, the amount was not vested as of the time of the arbitration award. The request to specify the amount to be awarded to each of the petitioners is denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CHANG AHN VS. KELSO MARTINIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment And Recall Writ Of Possession
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 10, 2010, line 13. DEFENDANT KELSO MARTINIS'S Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment And Recall Writ Of Possession is granted. Law favors judgment on the merits. Defendant demonstrates excusable mistake and has moved timely to set aside the default. Defendant must pay costs of $150. Defendant must file his answer on or before September 13, 2010. =(302/CWW)
